rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF MERLE ELYSE SHAPIRO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Conservator requests approval to hold 9,340.446 shares of T. Rowe Price Retirement 2020 Fund, which, as of the end of the account period has a market value of $186,248.50. 2) Conservator holds ~273 shares of MMC Stock Fund ($15,254.80 FMV) and ~11,150 shares of Investco Fixed Income ($273,490 FMV) but has not addressed whether these are appropriate investments for conservatorships. 3) Conservator requests authority to add designated beneficiaries to Conservatee's existing qualified accounts. 4) Attorney requests $44,103 for their service and requests that the trustee pays these fees. 5) Proof of Payment of Assessment has not been filed as of this review. Remote appearances are highly encouraged. Please review the 6th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 6th amended general order.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ABDUL AZIZ KHAN AND HAJRA NIGHAT KHAN JOINT LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, FEES
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 09/29/2020. 1) No account statements verifying the opening and ending balances have been filed. 2) A supplement signed and verified by the petitioner or other evidence addressing the following issues must be filed: A) Has the Exemption Trust been created? B) Have the reported assets been allocated between the Survivor's Trust and Marital Trust? C) Is the petitioner waiving trustee compensation? D) Paragraph 7 of the petition refers to the "SNT". E) Do Yasmeen Khan, Shehla Khan, and Rehan Khan share expenses for the trust real properties? F) According to the trust amendment, Rehan Kahn is entitled to be reimbursed from the trust residue for the cost of improvements to the Olmstead property. Should this be characterized as a liability of the trust and disclosed on Schedule H? G) There are no additional schedules for the Mangels and Olmstead properties for which specific beneficiaries have been designated. Probate Code section 1063(d). H) The petition does not request relief as to how the assets on hand should be distributed, nor does the petition state whether the assets have been delivered to the successor trustee (though no receipts are on file). I) Schedule A does not show any rental income from the trust real properties. J) Reinvested dividend amounts stated on Schedules A & G are different with respect to: i) Material Select Sector SPDR EFT; ii) Mototola Solutions; iii) Portland General Electric; iv) Select Sector Financial SPDR ETF; and v) Vanguard Utilities ETF. K) Why have expenses of the trust real properties been paid from the conservatorship estate? Schedule C does not show any expenses. L) According to Schedules B & G, 152 shares of Lumentum stock were purchased, and 0.64 shares were sold, but Schedule E shows 152 shares as of the account ending date. How did the trust acquire the 0.64 shares? M) As to Exhibit A: i) Is JB's work on 03/15/2019 related to the conservatorship? ii) Is MM's work on 08/08/2018 related to the conservatorship? iii) Should MM's work in October 2018 related to conflict waivers be compensated from this trust? iv) Why is MM's hourly rate $225 instead of $95 for his accounting work on 02/10/2020 & 06/19/2020? v) Is part of RCN's work on 02/25/2019, 02/26/2019, & 05/28/2019 related to the conservatorship? vi) RCN's work on 06/25/2020 and File & ServeXpress service fee of $5 on 07/01/2020 appear unrelated to the petitioner. N) The attorney's declaration satisfying the requirements of CRC 7.703(e)(4)&(5) must be filed for the paralegals' services.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF BARBARA DEE WILSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Compliance Status Hearing
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Issues may include (but not limited to): Conservator's failure to file: inventory & appraisal with proof of mailing & notice of how to file objections; general plan & determination of appropriate level of care; and proof of recording of letters (if estate includes real property). ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Counsel and parties may appear by video or audio-only telephone through Zoom. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Review the Court's 6th Amended General Order which took effect on 07/21/2020 for more information. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE VICTORIA CHOU TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For An Order To Confirm Successor Trustee And For Instructions Re Distribution Of Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/6/20. An amended petition was filed on 8/27/20, which was not at least 30 days before this hearing. [CRC 7.53(a).] No courtesy copy of the amended petition or proof of service has been received by the assigned Examiner as of 9/3/20. [LRSF 2.7 B., 14.6, and 14.93 G.] It appears that the incorrect form of Notice was used again.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE VICTORIA CHOU TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition By Administrator With Will Annexed To Establish Estates Claim Of Ownership To Real Property And For Order Directing Its Transfer To Estate; Removal Of Trustee, And Accounting With Exhibits 1-7 [Probate Code 850, 17200]
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/6/20 to be heard with the competing petition and also because there is no such thing as a "supplement correcting" a pleading. [CRC 7.3.] An amendment corrects errors in a pleading. Notice is required for an amendment. [CRC 7.53(a).] This is not a Civil "action" it is a trust proceeding in which petitions have been filed and for which the mandatory Judicial Council form of Notice of Hearing is required to be served.
